shape_semantics: vp-PRO + vpc-anti. code over (cmp,vp,vpc,naive) = L,W,L,_ : value_profile RESOLVES; cmplog AND value_profile_cmplog both BLOCK; naive non-decisive. The decisive comparison is value_profile > value_profile_cmplog (the I2S delta HURTS): adding the I2SRandReplace stage to the value-profile feedback REGRESSES the pair (vp 9/10 resolved vs vpc 8/10 blocked, 18.00h blocked). Both branches are libxml2 (sB-server targets; sB holds libxml2's campaign + the prompts_a reps' seeds + the libxml2 cov image, so sB arbitrates both). ROUND HISTORY: round-0 led with the vp-PRO half via a NEW value_distance_reached descriptor (byte-DISTANCE-to-operand) and was REFUTED on both branches (rule_not_met) -- value-distance does NOT discriminate this shape; it is DEAD here and is NOT re-proposed. Round-2 added the vpc-ANTI I2S-literal-FLOOD diversion via the BUILT seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count (joint_necessity) token-count descriptor (KEPT verbatim) and the vpc-ANTI sibling-subtree EXECUTION-DEPTH diversion via the BUILT depth_reach descriptor (KEPT verbatim). The depth-diversion hypothesis CONFIRMED libxml2/6837 (it is now a FROZEN confirmed branch). libxml2/6343 remains UNLABELED: neither the token-flood nor the depth-diversion rule held there (rule_not_met). THIS ROUND (final) adds ONE genuinely-different mechanism aimed at 6343: not how MANY whole literal copies the corpus carries (token-flood) and not whether the corpus REACHES the branch line (depth), but whether, AT THE FIXED 4-BYTE GATE WINDOW ITSELF, the vpc corpus is DEPLETED of the exact target byte pattern (the 00 00 3C 00 run the gradient assembles) because I2S splices SIBLING-encoding decoy comparands into those same offsets and displaces the 0x00-run. This is the BUILT operand_enrichment / byte_freq_ratio descriptor (not yet tried on this shape), in its ANTI direction (signed_target_enrich < 0), pointed at the vpc-vs-vp arms.

hypotheses.label: Adding I2S in vpc intercepts the gate's own literal comparands and SPLICES them back into inputs, flooding the vpc corpus with copies of the gate's exact literals (encoding magics for 6343; '<![CDATA[','<?xml','<!DOCTYPE' for 6837). The vpc-BLOCKING seeds are therefore ENRICHED in these diverting literal tokens relative to the vp-RESOLVING seeds, which took the diverse / false-side path that the CMP_MAP gradient navigates. The I2S flood is the net liability that regresses vpc vs vp.

params.comment: Use joint_necessity in its GENERIC analyze_pair mode (NOT the 3-arm joint analyze): winner_fz=value_profile, loser_fz=value_profile_cmplog. The tool counts, per role's seed set, how many of --tokens (the gate's diverting literal comparands) appear per seed: vp_tags = mean token count over vp-RESOLVING seeds, vpc_tags = mean token count over vpc-BLOCKING seeds, tag_lift = vp_tags - vpc_tags. The I2S-flood diversion predicts vpc seeds carry MORE of the gate's literals than vp seeds (tag_lift < 0). This is a TOKEN-COUNT (corpus-accumulation) signal, distinct from round-0's refuted byte-DISTANCE-to-operand: distance asks 'how close to the operand', token_count asks 'how many copies of the diverting literals the corpus accreted' -- the flood signature itself.

tokens_rationale.libxml2/6343: The 4-byte encoding-detection magics tested around encoding.c:958 (UCS4 variants 00 00 3C 00 / 00 00 00 3C / 3C 00 00 00 / 00 3C 00 00, the UTF16 BOMs FF FE / FE FF) plus the '<?xml' literal -- the exact comparands xmlDetectCharEncoding logs that I2S would splice. The vpc seeds, flooded with completions of the SIBLING UTF16BE/other-encoding gates (the [L] line-980 path), should carry these magics; vp's gradient-driven seeds need only the 00-run window, not the full sibling-magic literals.
tokens_rationale.libxml2/6837: The exact multi-byte literals the [L] CDATA/declaration paths compare (parser.c:11747-11754 '<![CDATA['; the '<?xml' decl L11562-11564; '<!DOCTYPE'). The vpc-blocking seeds funnel into these true-side exact-literal completions (the I2S flood), while the vp-resolving seeds diversify content to ride the FALSE side and never complete the literal.

params.tau_tag_note: tau_tag / tau_size are not used by the analyze_pair rule below (they gate the 3-arm joint verdict, not the pair metrics); the rule is expressed directly over vp_tags / vpc_tags / tag_lift.
params.false_side_note_6837: 6837's W side is the FALSE side of the CDATA AND gate, which makes the orientation CONSISTENT with the flood story without a sign flip: the vp-resolving (false-side) seeds AVOID the literal, the vpc-blocking seeds CARRY it, so vpc_tags > vp_tags is the natural prediction for BOTH branches.
params.feasibility: joint_necessity (descriptor seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count:tokens:per_branch) is ALREADY BUILT + registered (anchor harfbuzz/6814, validated). It consumes seed_set (resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds in db/blockers.sqlite) read from the on-disk queue; it does NOT need corpus_queue and does NOT need the I2S mutation_op tag (it counts literal tokens in seed BYTES, never an 'I2S event'). On THIS server (s4) libxml2's seeds/queue are off-server, so analyze_pair returns skip (insufficient seeds) and s4 leaves both branches for sB. On sB (which holds libxml2's campaign + the 286 prompts_a reps' seeds) both branches are scorable. Per data_realities this is compliant: NOT seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence (I2S untagged), NOT corpus_queue:byte_freq_ratio (these are multi-byte literal gates with different-natured W/L seeds where byte-offset alignment is unreliable -- token PRESENCE counting sidesteps alignment).

hypotheses.prediction: If the vpc-anti mechanism is the I2S literal-FLOOD diversion, the vpc-BLOCKING seeds carry MORE copies of the gate's own diverting literals than the vp-RESOLVING seeds: vpc_tags > vp_tags so tag_lift < 0, with a real (not marginal) gap (vpc_tags meaningfully above 0 AND above vp_tags). DISCRIMINATING alternatives this rules out: (a) if the vp-resolving seeds carry the literals just as much (tag_lift ~ 0, vp_tags ~ vpc_tags), then vpc is NOT diverted by a literal flood -- it regresses for some other reason (e.g. plain scheduling noise), and the diversion story fails; (b) if vp_tags > vpc_tags (tag_lift > 0), the winner is the one carrying the literals, which inverts the flood story entirely (it would suggest the literals HELP, contradicting the L label on vpc). Only vpc_tags > vp_tags is consistent with 'I2S splices the gate's comparands into vpc's corpus and starves the false-side / sibling-diverse path vp rides'.
hypotheses.rule: vpc_tags > vp_tags AND tag_lift <= -0.5 AND vpc_tags >= 0.5
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable_by: tag_lift >= 0 (vp-resolving seeds carry the diverting literals at least as much as the vpc-blocking seeds) OR vpc_tags < 0.5 (vpc's corpus is NOT actually flooded with the gate literals) refutes the I2S-literal-flood diversion. Either outcome points away from 'I2S floods vpc with the gate's own comparands' toward a non-flood reason vpc regresses (scheduling, gradient-energy split with no token signature).
hypotheses.g2_independence: Token-presence counts over role seed sets are independent of the resolve pattern that DEFINED the shape: the measurement does not ask 'does value_profile resolve / does vpc block' (the W/L label-source); it asks 'how many copies of the gate's literal comparands does each role's corpus carry', a structural property of the seed bytes orthogonal to the resolve labels. The label-SOURCE (analysis decisive-shape) and the verification-SIGNAL (literal token density in seeds) are distinct.

hypotheses.label: The I2S liability in vpc manifests as EXECUTION-DEPTH diversion at the target branch line: the vpc-BLOCKING corpus, having spliced the gate's logged comparands, gets funneled into the SIBLING / true-side state-machine sub-tree (6343: the sibling UTF16BE encoding gate at encoding.c:980; 6837: the CDATA-section + '<?xml'/'<!DOCTYPE' declaration state machines), so its seeds rarely REACH and execute the target branch line (6343: encoding.c:958; 6837: parser.c:11754) and pile up on the blocked side. The vp-RESOLVING corpus instead rides the shallow false-side / general-parse path, reaching and executing the target branch line per seed. The signature is winner_depth > loser_depth at the branch line: vp seeds reach the branch, vpc seeds got diverted before it.

params.comment: Run depth_reach with winner = value_profile-RESOLVING seeds, loser = value_profile_cmplog-BLOCKING seeds through the libafl-libxml2-cov image. The tool reads, at the target branch LINE, the per-seed-normalized execution count (depth) and the blocked-side count from llvm-cov. winner_depth = per-seed exec count of the target branch line over the vp-resolving seed set; loser_depth = same over the vpc-blocking seed set; depth_lift = winner_depth / loser_depth (or winner-loser normalized per the tool); side_lift compares blocked-side counts. The DIVERSION mechanism predicts the vpc-blocking corpus barely reaches the target line (loser_depth low) while the vp-resolving corpus executes it (winner_depth high), with winner_deeper=true.

branch_line_by_branch.libxml2/6343: /src/libxml2/encoding.c:958 (xmlDetectCharEncoding, the 4-byte UCS4_2143 detect window). The SIBLING diversion target is encoding.c:980 (the [L]-tagged UTF16BE gate the loser seeds resolve instead) -- the loser corpus reaching 980 but not 958 is the diversion footprint; depth_reach scores the TARGET line 958.
branch_line_by_branch.libxml2/6837: /src/libxml2/parser.c:11754 (the 9-clause CDATA AND gate, cur[8]=='['). The W side is the FALSE side of this AND gate. The loser corpus is diverted into the [L] CDATA-section / '<?xml' / '<!DOCTYPE' sub-trees (L11555-11591, L11813-11984); reaching those deep paths instead of executing the general-parse false-side at 11754 is the diversion.

params.false_side_orientation_6837: 6837's winner side is the FALSE side of the AND gate. depth_reach measures EXECUTION of the branch LINE (how many times each role's seeds drive the parser past line 11754), NOT which side they take -- so the false-side orientation does NOT invert the prediction: the vp-resolving seeds advance through the general-parse content and re-execute the branch-line region (high winner_depth), while the vpc-blocking seeds get pinned in the CDATA/declaration sub-trees and stop re-entering the general START_TAG/CONTENT loop that re-evaluates 11754 (low loser_depth). The metric is winner_deeper at the branch line, consistent across both branches.
params.why_orthogonal_to_token_flood: The token-count hypothesis (vpc_anti_literal_flood_token_enrichment) measures CORPUS COMPOSITION (how many literal copies each role's seeds carry). depth_reach measures EXECUTION REACH (does the loser corpus actually drive the target branch line, or did it stall in a sibling sub-tree). These can come apart: (a) the vpc corpus could be enriched in literals yet still re-reach the branch line (token-flood PASS, depth FAIL) -- the literals would not then explain the regression; (b) the vpc corpus could be depth-diverted WITHOUT a clean literal-count gap (depth PASS, token-flood FAIL) -- e.g. the diversion is into a sibling gate whose comparand is not in the token list. A branch confirmed by depth but not token (or vice versa) is genuinely a different mechanism, so the two are kept separate and tried in order.
params.feasibility: depth_reach (descriptor seed_set:depth_reach:branch_line:per_branch) is ALREADY BUILT + registered (anchor harfbuzz/6585, validated). It needs (1) the target's cov image -- libafl-libxml2-cov, whose Dockerfile.libxml2.cov EXISTS, and (2) resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds in db/blockers.sqlite (both branches are prompts_a reps with seeds). It is Docker per-branch (~30-60s) and reads on-disk seeds, so it scores ONLY where the seeds + cov image are local: sB holds libxml2's seeds and can build its cov image, so sB arbitrates both branches; s4 (libxml2 off-server) returns skip and leaves them for sB. Per data_realities this is compliant: it uses seed_set EXECUTION (llvm-cov line counts), NOT seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence (I2S untagged) and NOT corpus_queue:byte_freq_ratio (no byte-offset alignment needed -- it counts line executions, alignment-free).

hypotheses.prediction: If the vpc regression is an EXECUTION-DEPTH diversion (I2S funnels the vpc corpus into the sibling/true-side sub-tree, away from the target branch line), the vp-RESOLVING seeds execute the target branch line markedly MORE per seed than the vpc-BLOCKING seeds: winner_depth > loser_depth, depth_lift well above 1, winner_deeper=true. DISCRIMINATING alternatives this rules out: (a) if loser_depth ~ winner_depth (both corpora reach the branch line equally), then vpc is NOT diverted before the branch -- it reaches the gate and fails for a value reason, NOT a path-diversion reason, so the sibling-subtree-diversion story fails (and the token-flood / value mechanisms would be the live ones instead); (b) if loser_depth > winner_depth (vpc reaches the line MORE), the diversion story is inverted -- vpc is not starved of the branch region at all. Only winner_depth > loser_depth (vp reaches the branch, vpc got diverted before it) is consistent with 'I2S funnels vpc into the sibling state machine and starves the general-parse path vp rides to the branch'.
hypotheses.rule: winner_deeper == true AND depth_lift >= 1.5 AND winner_depth > loser_depth
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable_by: depth_lift < 1.5 (the vpc-blocking corpus reaches the target branch line about as often as the vp-resolving corpus) OR winner_deeper == false (vpc reaches it at least as much) refutes the sibling-subtree-depth-diversion mechanism -- the vpc regression is then NOT a before-the-branch path diversion but a value/scheduling effect at or beyond the gate. This is a different prediction from the token-flood test (which a flooded-but-still-reaching corpus would pass while this depth test fails), so the two cannot both be vacuously satisfied.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Per-seed execution count of a fixed source branch LINE is independent of the resolve pattern that DEFINED the shape: the measurement does not ask 'does value_profile resolve / does vpc block' (the W/L label-source from the analysis); it asks 'how many times does each role's corpus drive the parser through the target branch line', a structural execution property orthogonal to the resolve labels. The label-SOURCE (analysis decisive-shape) and the verification-SIGNAL (llvm-cov line-execution depth) are distinct.

hypotheses.label: At the FIXED 4-byte encoding-detection window itself (encoding.c:958, the 00 00 3C 00 UCS4_2143 magic), the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient assembles the target 0x00-run + 0x3C byte-by-byte, so vp-RESOLVING seeds carry the target byte pattern at those exact offsets. Adding I2S in vpc splices SIBLING-encoding decoy comparands (the other 4-byte magics 00 00 00 3C / 3C 00 00 00, the FF FE / FE FF BOMs) into those SAME head offsets, DISPLACING the gradient-assembled 0x00-run -- so the vpc-BLOCKING corpus is DEPLETED of the target pattern at the gate offsets (and ENRICHED in the decoy pattern). The signature is a NEGATIVE per-offset byte enrichment of vpc over vp at the gate window: signed_target_enrich < 0 with decoy_enrich > 0 -- the canonical I2S-ANTI byte-availability footprint. This is distinct from how MANY whole literal copies the seed carries (token-flood) and from whether the corpus reaches the branch LINE (depth): it asks whether, AT the gate window, the exact resolving bytes are present.

params.comment: Run operand_enrichment (bench/tools/i2s_operand_availability.py) in `branch` mode with --winner-fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --loser-fuzzer value_profile. The tool AUTO-DISCOVERS the gate signature: it reads the branch's W=resolving and L=blocking seeds from db/blockers.sqlite, finds the head offsets where W and L systematically disagree at >= --purity (W-consensus byte = TARGET pattern = the 00 00 3C 00 magic the gradient assembles; L-consensus byte = DECOY pattern = the sibling-encoding bytes). It then measures, over the vpc corpus vs the vp corpus, the per-offset byte fraction matching TARGET vs DECOY, as log2(target_frac_vpc / target_frac_vp) = signed_target_enrich and log2(decoy_frac_vpc / decoy_frac_vp) = decoy_enrich. The tool's column names retain cmplog_/naive_ for backward-compat but are arm-agnostic (winner=vpc, loser=vp here). The ANTI prediction: I2S in vpc displaces the gradient-assembled target bytes with sibling-magic decoys -> signed_target_enrich < 0 (vpc DEPLETED of the target pattern at the gate offsets) AND decoy_enrich > 0 (vpc ENRICHED in the decoy sibling bytes). Use --head 16 (the encoding window is at the input head) and --purity 0.6 (the registry default).
params.arm_orientation_note: Deliberately winner=value_profile_cmplog (the I2S-ADDED, BLOCKING arm) and loser=value_profile (the I2S-FREE, RESOLVING arm). This isolates the I2S CONTRIBUTION over the VP-only blocker exactly as the tool's docstring prescribes for vpc-only / I2S-hurts shapes ('pass winner=value_profile_cmplog, loser=value_profile to isolate the I2S contribution over the VP-only blocker'). signed_target_enrich is then log2(vpc/vp) of the target-byte fraction at the gate offsets -- NEGATIVE means I2S DEPLETED the resolving bytes, the I2S-anti signature. (Note: 'winner'/'loser' in the tool's arg names are arm LABELS, not the W/L resolve roles; here the I2S-added arm is named winner-fuzzer to anchor the log2 numerator.)
params.applicability_6343: 6343 is a FIXED-OFFSET 4-byte VALUE gate (xmlDetectCharEncoding reads start[0..3] at the input head), with a contiguous_literal byte_signature and operand_width_bytes=4 -- exactly the 'fixed-offset VALUE gates' the operand_enrichment tool applies_to (pro enrich>0 / anti deplete<0). It is NOT the structural-assembly / different-sized-seed case that data_realities forbids byte_freq_ratio for: the gate window is a fixed head offset, so per-offset byte alignment is well-defined. The auto-discovered W/L consensus split (00-run target vs sibling-magic decoy) is the gate signature the card describes ('winner-resolving seeds carry runs of 0x00 bytes ... that the gradient feedback assembled into the magic prefix').
params.not_applicable_6837: This hypothesis is SCOPED to 6343 only. 6837's gate (parser.c:11754, a 9-clause AND whose W side is the FALSE side, with the '![CDATA[' literal compared mid-stream not at a fixed head offset) is NOT a clean fixed-head-offset value window -- its resolving seeds AVOID a mid-stream literal rather than carrying a fixed-offset target byte run, so an auto-discovered head-offset consensus gate is ill-defined there. 6837 is already FROZEN/CONFIRMED by the depth-diversion hypothesis; this byte-depletion test is not applied to it (member_hint_branches lists 6343 only).
params.feasibility: operand_enrichment (descriptor corpus_queue:byte_freq_ratio:target_vs_decoy:per_gate_offset) is ALREADY BUILT + registered (anchor harfbuzz/5391, validated: pro median +3.12 / anti -0.55, 88% threshold separation). It reads the branch's resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds (to discover the gate signature) AND the on-disk saved CORPORA for the two arms (value_profile_cmplog, value_profile) from out/<target>/<fuzzer>/trial<N>/queue. Both are present on sB (libxml2's campaign + the prompts_a reps' seeds); s4 (libxml2 off-server) returns skip (no_gate_signature / missing corpus) and leaves 6343 for sB. Per data_realities this is compliant: it is corpus_queue:byte_freq_ratio applied to a FIXED-OFFSET value gate (the case the forbidding caveat explicitly EXEMPTS -- the caveat forbids byte_freq_ratio only for joint/structural-assembly gates with different-SIZED W/L seeds; 6343's encoding window is a fixed head offset). It does NOT use seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence (I2S untagged) -- the I2S effect is inferred from corpus byte AVAILABILITY at the gate offsets, never a lineage 'I2S event'.

hypotheses.prediction: If the 6343 vpc regression is I2S DISPLACING the gradient-assembled target bytes at the gate window with sibling-encoding decoys, then over the gate offsets the vpc corpus is DEPLETED of the target 00 00 3C 00 pattern relative to the vp corpus AND enriched in the decoy sibling bytes: signed_target_enrich < 0 (clearly negative, near the validated anti band ~ -0.55) AND decoy_enrich > 0. DISCRIMINATING alternatives this rules out: (a) if signed_target_enrich >= 0 (the vpc corpus carries the target bytes at the gate offsets at least as much as vp), then I2S did NOT deplete the resolving bytes at the window -- the byte-displacement story fails and the regression is not a gate-window availability effect (it would then be a pure scheduling/energy split with no byte footprint, or the already-tested flood/depth mechanisms); (b) if decoy_enrich <= 0 (vpc is not enriched in the sibling-decoy bytes either), then I2S is not splicing the sibling magics into the head window at all, refuting the displacement story. Only signed_target_enrich < 0 with decoy_enrich > 0 is consistent with 'I2S harvests sibling-encoding comparands and splices them into the head window, displacing the 0x00-run vp's gradient needs'. This is a DIFFERENT prediction from the token-flood test (whole-literal COUNT anywhere in the seed) and the depth test (branch-LINE execution): a corpus can carry sibling literals somewhere (token-flood PASS) yet still hold the target bytes at the gate offsets (this test FAIL), or be depth-diverted (depth PASS) without a clean per-offset byte swap (this test FAIL) -- so this is not a relabel of either prior.
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable_by: signed_target_enrich > -0.4 (the vpc corpus is NOT depleted of the target gate bytes -- it carries the 00 00 3C 00 pattern at the gate offsets about as much as, or more than, the vp corpus) OR decoy_enrich < 0 (vpc is not enriched in the sibling-decoy bytes at those offsets) refutes the gate-window byte-displacement mechanism. Either outcome means I2S is not displacing the gradient-assembled resolving bytes at the encoding window, so the 6343 vpc regression is not a gate-window operand-availability effect -- it falls through to inconclusive rather than being force-labeled.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Per-offset byte-frequency enrichment over the two arms' corpora is independent of the resolve pattern that DEFINED the shape: the measurement does not ask 'does value_profile resolve / does vpc block' (the W/L label-source from the analysis). The gate signature (which offsets, target vs decoy byte) is discovered from the SEEDS' byte disagreement, and the enrichment is measured over the saved CORPORA's bytes -- a structural byte-availability property orthogonal to the resolve labels. The label-SOURCE (analysis decisive-shape) and the verification-SIGNAL (per-offset target-byte fraction in the saved corpora) are distinct.

notes: FINAL (round-3) RE-DESIGN of i2s_vp_LWL_ (2 branches, both libxml2 / sB targets). STATE: libxml2/6837 is FROZEN/CONFIRMED (vpc_anti_sibling_subtree_depth_diversion held there). libxml2/6343 was still UNLABELED (rule_not_met on BOTH prior rules). PRIORS KEPT VERBATIM (superset/monotonic): both vpc_anti_literal_flood_token_enrichment (joint_necessity token-count, decidable:true) and vpc_anti_sibling_subtree_depth_diversion (depth_reach, decidable:true, confirms 6837) are carried UNCHANGED and remain FIRST and SECOND in decision_order so the confirmed branch re-validates and nothing is dropped or relaxed. PRIORS NOT RE-PROPOSED: round-0's value_distance_reached (byte-DISTANCE-to-operand) was REFUTED on both branches and is DEAD here -- not re-tried (relaxed-threshold re-test forbidden). NEW MECHANISM (this round, scoped to the still-unlabeled 6343): vpc_anti_gate_window_target_byte_depletion -- a genuinely DIFFERENT mechanism from both kept priors and from the dead value-distance. It uses the BUILT operand_enrichment / byte_freq_ratio descriptor (corpus_queue:byte_freq_ratio:target_vs_decoy:per_gate_offset, anchor harfbuzz/5391, validated; NOT yet tried on this shape), pointed at the vpc-vs-vp arms in its ANTI direction. NOVELTY/ORTHOGONALITY (G1): the three vpc-anti hypotheses measure three orthogonal things -- (1) token-flood = how MANY whole literal copies the seed carries ANYWHERE; (2) depth = whether the corpus EXECUTES the branch LINE; (3) gate-window depletion = whether, AT the fixed gate offsets, the EXACT target bytes are present vs displaced by decoys. A corpus can fail one and pass another, so the byte-depletion test is not a relabel of either prior, and it directly attacks 6343's distinctive card claim (the 0x00-run the gradient assembles being diverted by sibling-encoding I2S splices) that neither whole-literal-count nor branch-line-depth captured. DECISION ORDER: flood, then depth, then byte-depletion -- arbiter tries each, then falls to inconclusive. DECIDABLE / CROSS-SERVER (rule 4): ALL THREE hypotheses decidable:true; 6343 is NOT marked decidable:false for being off-server on s4 -- sB owns libxml2's campaign + the prompts_a reps' resolving/blocking seeds + the saved value_profile/value_profile_cmplog corpora + can build libafl-libxml2-cov, so sB arbitrates every hypothesis for both branches. NO honest decidable:false sub-group remains: 6343's distinctive 0x00-run-displacement mechanism IS measurable on sB via the fixed-offset byte-enrichment tool (the one avenue priors had not exploited); the only dead avenue (value-distance) is refuted, not undecidable. HONESTY (G3): if sB finds none of the three rules met on 6343 (no token-flood gap, no depth diversion, no gate-window byte depletion), 6343 falls through to inconclusive rather than being force-labeled -- an honest negative for the I2S-liability story on that branch. DATA-REALITY COMPLIANCE: the new byte-freq test is applied ONLY to 6343, a FIXED-OFFSET 4-byte value gate at the input head (the case the byte_freq_ratio-forbidding caveat explicitly EXEMPTS -- the caveat forbids it only for joint/structural-assembly gates with different-SIZED W/L seeds); it is NOT applied to 6837 (mid-stream AND-gate literal, no clean fixed-head-offset window -- and 6837 is already confirmed). No hypothesis uses seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence (I2S untagged); the I2S-liability story is inferred from corpus composition (token density), execution reach (line depth), and gate-window byte availability (per-offset target/decoy fraction), never from a lineage tag.
data_realities_respected: I2S is NOT tagged in mutation_op -- no hypothesis uses seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence on an 'I2S event'; the I2S-liability story is inferred from corpus composition (token density), execution reach (line depth), and gate-window byte availability (per-offset target/decoy fraction), never from a lineage tag.

value_profile (CMP_MAP) is a FEEDBACK not a mutator -- its effect is read via what the corpus reaches/holds (token density, branch-line execution depth, gate-window target-byte fraction), never a 'gradient event'.

byte_freq_ratio (operand_enrichment) is applied ONLY to 6343, a FIXED-OFFSET 4-byte value gate at the input head where per-offset alignment is well-defined -- NOT to joint/structural-assembly gates with different-SIZED W/L seeds (the case data_realities forbids it for); 6837 (mid-stream AND-gate literal) is excluded from the byte-freq test and the alignment-free token/depth tests cover it.

on-disk corpora / seeds + the cov image for libxml2 exist on sB (not s4): s4 skips both branches (off-server), sB arbitrates all hypotheses for both branches. Per rule 4 the branches are decidable:true, not decidable:false-for-absence.

both branches are prompts_a reps with resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds in db/blockers.sqlite; the token tool consumes the seed sets, depth_reach consumes seeds + the cov image, and operand_enrichment consumes the seeds (for the gate signature) + the saved value_profile / value_profile_cmplog corpora.
